fields.title: agent: enforce parent-only ACL on send/close
fields.description: Only the agent that spawned a child should be able to send messages to it or close it. Prevents cross-agent interference in multi-agent orchestrations.nnerg-effect reference: AgentManagerLive.send/close check canRequesterMutateAgent(actor, requesterAgentId). AgentAccessDeniedError is returned on violation.
fields.design: Add AgentAccessDenied tagged error in services.ts. Add optional requesterAgentId parameter to send and close on the AgentManager interface. In manager.send and manager.shutdown, look up the target's parentId and compare against requesterAgentId. If mismatch, fail with AgentAccessDenied. Thread the current agent's id from the tool layer (tool.ts) through AgentControl into manager calls. The orchestrator (main session) can always send/close (no requesterAgentId = no restriction, same as erg-effect).
fields.acceptance_criteria: A sub-agent cannot close or send to another sub-agent that it did not spawn. The orchestrator (main agent) retains full access. Violation returns a clear error message with the requester and parent ids.
fields.notes: Finalized ACL enforcement: manager shutdown now checks requester against parent map using helper canMutate(parentMapRef, targetId, requesterAgentId). Control send now denies cross-parent mutations and returns AgentAccessDenied. Tool send/close pass context.parentAgentId and map AgentAccessDenied to user-facing error text. Re-ran npm run gate in extensions/tau; typecheck/lint/test pass (lint warnings are pre-existing).
fields.close_reason: Implemented and reviewed. ACL enforced on send/close with AgentAccessDenied error.
